description: Rewrite html.ts to emit CSS Grid HTML with Unicode chord typography and full musical structure
detail:
Rewrite the HTML emission in html.ts using the layout data from computeGlobalLayout(). This covers all rendering changes in one pass of the file: (1) render() calls computeGlobalLayout first and emits --beat-cols and --min-beat-width on part="song"; (2) renderRow() uses the layout map to emit style="grid-column: C" on barlines and style="grid-column: C / span S" on slots; (3) renderChord() is rewritten to split the root into note + accidental (wrapping ♭/♯ in a chord-accidental span), map quality to Unicode symbols (△ ° ø), wrap quality in a chord-quality span, and handle chord.bass as fraction layout with chord-top / chord-fraction-line / chord-bass; (4) dot slots emit /; (5) the first slot of a bar emits a time-sig block when bar.timeSignature is set; (6) renderFrontMatter() becomes
with h1/p elements for title, artist (if present), key in normalised form (if present); (7) sections wrap in
with

(omitted when label is null); (8) barlines carry their kind as an additional part token (e.g. part="barline barline-single") and emit a barline-repeat-count child span when repeatCount > 2; (9) comment rendering is removed entirely. See project/plans/default-html-renderer.md for full context.
steps: Read the current html.ts and the plan file for the exact HTML structure expected
Update render() to call computeGlobalLayout and emit CSS variable inline styles on part="song"
Rewrite renderFrontMatter() as the new <header part="song-header"> structure
Update section rendering: <section part="section" style="display: contents">, h2 section-label (omit when null), remove comment rendering
Rewrite renderRow() to iterate the layout map, emitting barlines and slots with grid-column styles
Emit time-sig block in the first slot of each bar when bar.timeSignature is set
Emit dot slots as <span part="dot" style="grid-column: ..."> with / content
Rewrite renderChord(): accidentals → chord-accidental with ♭/♯; quality → Unicode symbol in chord-quality span; slash chord → fraction layout
Emit barline kind as second part token; add barline-repeat-count child when repeatCount > 2
Run pnpm test and fix any failures
Commit the change

description: Rewrite CSS in html-element.ts for CSS Grid layout and full musical typography
detail: Replace the existing monospace/pre-wrap styles in html-element.ts with a complete CSS Grid implementation. Changes: (1) update :host custom properties to the new set (--grigson-font-family, --grigson-row-gap, --grigson-section-gap, --grigson-barline-width, --grigson-barline-color, --grigson-repeat-dot-size, --grigson-title-font-size, --grigson-section-label-font-size, --grigson-time-sig-font-size); (2) style part="song-grid" as a CSS Grid with repeat(var(--beat-cols), minmax(var(--min-beat-width), 1fr)) plus a trailing 0fr column for the final barline anchor; (3) part="section" → display: contents; (4) part="section-label" → grid-column: 1 / -1, margin-top: var(--grigson-section-gap), with first-child margin reset to 0; (5) part="row" → grid-column: 1 / -1, display: grid, grid-template-columns: subgrid; (6) barline styles: width 0, border-left, align-self: stretch; barline-double box-shadow; barline-final thick border; startRepeat/endRepeat pseudo-element dot pairs; endRepeatStartRepeat combined; barline-repeat-count positioned above; (7) chord-quality: font-size 0.75em, vertical-align 0.25em; chord-accidental: font-size 0.8em, vertical-align 0.15em; (8) slash chord: chord-slash inline-flex column, chord-fraction-line 1px bar, chord-bass 0.85em; (9) time-sig: inline-flex column, bold, 0.7em; (10) change default font to Georgia serif; (11) update dark mode. See project/plans/default-html-renderer.md for full context.
steps: Read packages/grigson/src/renderers/html-element.ts to understand the current CSS structure
Replace the :host custom properties block with the new set from the plan
Add song-grid CSS Grid styles (global grid with beat columns + trailing 0fr barline anchor column)
Add section (display: contents), section-label (full-width + section-gap), and row (subgrid) styles
Add all barline variant styles (single, double, final, startRepeat, endRepeat, endRepeatStartRepeat, repeat-count)
Add chord-quality, chord-accidental, chord-slash fraction layout, and time-sig styles
Update font to Georgia serif and update dark mode styles
Remove old monospace/pre-wrap styles that no longer apply
Run pnpm test and confirm all tests pass
Commit the change

description: Update and extend HTML renderer unit tests to cover the new CSS Grid layout and typography
detail: Update the existing HTML renderer tests to match the new HTML structure, and add new tests for all the behaviours introduced by the rewrite. New tests to add: (1) computeGlobalLayout returns correct globalMaxBeats and globalMinBeatWidth from a multi-section song; (2) grid column assignment for rows with mixed beat counts (e.g. A Man's A Man chorus rows of 16/10/12 beats); (3) slash chord renders as fraction layout with chord-top / chord-fraction-line / chord-bass parts; (4) dot slot renders as <span part="dot"> with / content and correct grid-column; (5) time signature annotation appears in first slot when bar.timeSignature is set; (6) Unicode symbol output — ♭ and ♯ accidentals, △ for major7, ° for diminished, ø for half-diminished; (7) section elements carry display:contents via the part="section" attribute. Update any existing snapshot or structure tests that break due to the new HTML. See project/plans/default-html-renderer.md for full context.

Also add a note in the normalizer documentation (or a short note in renderer.md) that bar.timeSignature must be set on the first bar for time signature annotations to appear. description: Add Math Bold cmap aliases and U+1D7CE–1D7D7 to the Bravura subset script
detail: Update scripts/gen-bravura-subset.mjs to add a Python preprocessing step before pyftsubset runs. The step opens Bravura.otf, iterates U+E080–E089, and adds cmap aliases at U+1D7CE–1D7D7 pointing to the same glyph names, saving a temp OTF. Then update the UNICODES constant to include the new range: 'U+266D,U+266F,U+E080-E08B,U+E500,U+E501,U+1D7CE-1D7D7'. No new glyphs are created — one set of outlines serves both codepoint ranges. See project/plans/bravura-time-signature-re-encode.md for full context.

description: Synthesize barlineDouble and barlineFinal glyphs in GrigsonJazz by constructing them from existing FinaleJazz outlines
detail: In scripts/reencode-finale-jazz.mjs, add two Python imports (TransformPen, BoundsPen) and a synthetic glyph section after the IMPORT loop: (1) barlineDouble (U+E031 / uniE031) — draw barlineSingle twice, with the second copy shifted right by single_adv + inter_stroke_gap, where the gap is measured from the two barline strokes inside repeatRight (uniE041); (2) barlineFinal (U+E032 / uniE032) — replay only the non-dot contours of repeatRight via a TransformPen that shifts them so the thin stroke aligns with x=0, keeping the same advance width as repeatRight. Dot contours are classified by aspect ratio: height < 3× width. Register both glyphs in CharStrings, hmtx, glyph order, and cmap (add SYNTHETIC_BMP = {0xE031: 'uniE031', 0xE032: 'uniE032'} and merge into bmp_import before the cmap update loop). In scripts/gen-jazz-subsets.mjs, extend the GrigsonJazz unicodes from U+E030 to U+E030-E032. See project/plans/add-glyphs-to-finale.md for full context.

Measure the inter-stroke gap from repeatRight (uniE041): record its contours, filter to non-dot strokes, sort by xMin, compute gap = xMin(stroke2) - xMax(stroke1)
Build barlineDouble (uniE031): record barlineSingle, replay it twice into a T2CharStringPen (second copy via TransformPen offset by single_adv + gap), register CharString, hmtx, glyph order
Build barlineFinal (uniE032): replay only the barline_pairs contours via a TransformPen that translates by single_xMin - thin_xMin, register CharString, hmtx, glyph order
Add SYNTHETIC_BMP = {0xE031: 'uniE031', 0xE032: 'uniE032'} and merge into bmp_import before the cmap update loop

Create `packages/website/content/renderers/ssr.njk` with frontmatter (`layout: base.njk`, `title: Build-time rendering`, `permalink: /renderers/build-time/`) and sections: intro, live demo using the shortcode, how-it-works explanation, code example using the existing `highlight` shortcode, font note

Add a bullet for the new page to the renderer list in `packages/website/content/renderers/index.md`

description: Add reflowSong and splitRows reflow functions to the HTML renderer
detail: Add barsPerLine and maxBarsPerLine to TextRendererConfig. Implement and export reflowSong() (flat reflow: flatten all section bars then rechunk into rows of N, ignoring source row breaks) and splitRows() (phrase-aware: split source rows that exceed N bars but never merge across row boundaries) in html.ts. Wire both into HtmlRenderer.render() so the correct transform is applied before computeGlobalLayout. Include unit tests for both functions covering: no-op, merging, splitting, short last row, open barline inheritance, and phrase-boundary preservation. See project/plans/reflow-renderer.md for full context.
steps: Add barsPerLine?: number and maxBarsPerLine?: number to TextRendererConfig in packages/grigson/src/renderers/text.ts

Implement and export reflowSong(song, barsPerLine) in html.ts: collect all bars per section, chunk into rows of N, first chunk inherits original open barline, subsequent chunks get { kind: 'single' }, content set to undefined
Implement and export splitRows(song, maxBarsPerLine) in html.ts: per source row, if bars.length > N split into chunks preserving open barline on first chunk; never merge across source rows
In HtmlRenderer.render(), apply reflowSong if barsPerLine is set, else splitRows if maxBarsPerLine is set, before calling computeGlobalLayout; use the transformed song for all subsequent iteration

Add describe('reflowSong') and describe('splitRows') test blocks in html.test.ts covering all cases from the plan table, plus a smoke test verifying part='row' count changes correctly for both config keys

description: Expose bars-per-line and max-bars-per-line attributes on GrigsonHtmlRenderer
detail: Add bars-per-line and max-bars-per-line to the observedAttributes list on GrigsonHtmlRenderer and parse them as positive integers in renderChart(), mapping to barsPerLine and maxBarsPerLine in the renderer config. Update the grigson package README to document both attributes alongside the existing ones. See project/plans/reflow-renderer.md for full context.
steps: Add 'bars-per-line' and 'max-bars-per-line' to observedAttributes in html-element.ts
In renderChart(), parse each attribute with parseInt and set config.barsPerLine / config.maxBarsPerLine if the parsed value is a positive integer
Update packages/grigson/README.md to document both new attributes with a short example showing the responsive layout pattern (bars-per-line for wide, max-bars-per-line for narrow)

description: Add source locations to all AST nodes in the grigson parser
detail: Extend the parser so every AST node carries an enumerable `loc?: SourceRange` property (0-based line/character, LSP-style). Add the `SourceRange` interface to `types.ts` and `loc?` to all node interfaces (Song, Section, Row, Bar, ChordSlot, DotSlot, Chord, FrontMatter, CommentLine). Add a `makeLoc` global-initializer helper to `grammar.pegjs` and call it in every relevant rule action; replace the existing non-enumerable `_sourceRange` on Bar nodes with the new `loc`. Track first/last item locs in the `SongBody` action to compute Section locs. Run `pnpm run build:grammar` to regenerate the parser. Update `validator.ts` to use `bar.loc` instead of `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(bar, '_sourceRange')`. Update parser tests that use `toEqual` on AST nodes to use `toMatchObject` so the extra `loc` property is ignored. Export `SourceRange` from `src/index.ts` and `src/index.browser.ts`. See project/plans/ast-explorer.md for full context.
steps: Add `SourceRange` interface and `loc?: SourceRange` fields to all node interfaces in `src/parser/types.ts`
Add a global initializer block with `makeLoc(l)` helper to the top of `src/parser/grammar.pegjs`
Add `loc: makeLoc(location())` to the return values of the Song, FrontMatter, Comment, SectionLabel, Row, BeatSlot, and Chord rules
Replace the `Object.defineProperty(_sourceRange …)` blocks in BarTail (both alternatives) and Bar with `bar.loc = makeLoc(location())`
Add section loc tracking to the SongBody action: record sectionStartLoc from the first item, update lastItemLoc on each item, assign loc when pushing each section
Run `pnpm run build:grammar` to regenerate `src/parser/generated.js` and `generated.d.ts`
Update `src/validator.ts`: replace the `barRange()` body with `return bar.loc ?? zeroRange()` and remove the now-unused `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` call

Update parser test files (`parser.test.ts`, `beatSlot.test.ts`, `section.test.ts`, `simile.test.ts`, `examples.test.ts`) — replace `toEqual` with `toMatchObject` on assertions that compare AST node shapes
Add `SourceRange` to the export lists in `src/index.ts` and `src/index.browser.ts`
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` from the repo root and confirm everything passes

description: Create the /ast-explorer/ page with Monaco editor, JSON tree view, and hover-to-highlight
detail: Add a new `/ast-explorer/` page to the website. Install `json-formatter-js` as a dev dependency in `packages/website` and add passthrough copies for its JS and CSS in `.eleventy.js`. Create `content/ast-explorer.njk` with a two-column layout (Monaco editor left, AST tree right). Load the same Monaco + grigson language/theme setup as the playground; use LZ-String to serialise editor content into the URL hash as `#code/{compressed}` (same format as playground). On each editor change, call `grigson.parseSong()`, pass the result to `JSONFormatter`, and render the tree. After rendering, recursively walk the AST and the rendered DOM in tandem to attach `mouseover`/`mouseout` handlers to every element corresponding to a node with a `loc` property; on hover use `editor.deltaDecorations()` to highlight the source range. On parse error, clear the tree and show the error message. Add an AST Explorer link to the nav in `_includes/base.njk`. Add a dynamic "View AST" link to the playground's editor toolbar that opens `/ast-explorer/` with the same `#code/…` hash. See project/plans/ast-explorer.md for full context.
steps: Add `json-formatter-js` to `packages/website/package.json` devDependencies and run `pnpm install`
Add passthrough copies for `json-formatter.min.js` and `json-formatter.css` to `.eleventy.js`
Create `content/ast-explorer.njk` with permalink `/ast-explorer/`, two-column layout, and the required `<script>`/`<link>` tags
Implement `readHash()` and Monaco editor initialisation (language registration, themes, options) — mirror the playground setup
Implement `update()`: parse with `grigson.parseSong()`, render tree with `JSONFormatter`, show parse errors
Implement `attachHoverHandlers(obj, containerEl)`: walk AST + DOM in tandem, attach `mouseover`/`mouseout` to nodes with `loc`, use `editor.deltaDecorations()` to highlight ranges
Implement `updateURL()`: debounced LZ-String compression into `history.replaceState` hash
Add the AST Explorer nav link to `_includes/base.njk`
Add a `#view-ast-link` anchor to the playground editor toolbar and update it inside `updateURL()` to carry the current hash
Run `pnpm build` and manually verify the page in a dev server: tree renders, hover highlights source, URL round-trips, error state clears tree, playground link works

description: Add TonalityHint grammar rule, Bar.tonalityHints type, and parser tests
detail: Add an inline `{Ab major}` / `{D dorian}` / `{}` / `{home}` tonality hint syntax to the Peggy grammar. Extract shared key-validation helpers (`isValidKey`, `normalizeKey`) into the `{{ }}` global initializer block, replacing duplicated inline logic in FrontMatter and SectionLabel. Add a `TonalityHint` grammar rule and a `BeatSlotItem` alternation so hints are parsed inline with beat slots but separated out into a `hints` array in the `BeatSlotList` action. Update `BarTail` to set `bar.tonalityHints` from that array. Add `TonalityHintItem` interface and `tonalityHints?` field to `Bar` in `types.ts`. Rebuild the grammar and add parser tests covering all valid hint forms, reset tokens, and rejection of invalid/shorthand forms. See project/plans/ast-explorer-with-harmony-analysis.md for full context.
steps: In grammar.pegjs global initializer block, extract VALID_NOTES, VALID_SUFFIXES, isValidKey(), and normalizeKey() helpers; replace inline validation in FrontMatter and SectionLabel with calls to these helpers
Add TonalityHint and TonalityHintKey grammar rules after TimeSignatureToken
Rename BeatSlot alternation to BeatSlotItem and add TonalityHint as the first alternative

Rewrite BeatSlotList action to separate hints from slots using beforeSlotIndex; move the 'must contain a chord' check into BeatSlotList; update BarTail to destructure { slots, hints } and set bar.tonalityHints when hints.length > 0
Run: cd packages/grigson && pnpm run build:grammar
Add TonalityHintItem interface and tonalityHints? field to Bar in types.ts
Add parser tests: {D minor} at bar start, {Ab major} mid-bar, all valid modes, {} and {home} reset, rejection of {Hb major} / {Dm} / {D}, bar with both time sig and hint

description: Implement AnalysedSong types, analyseSong(), and export from grigson package
detail: Add `loc?` to `AnnotatedChord` (copying from `chord.loc`) in `harmonicAnalysis.ts`, updating both `annotate()` and the borrowed-chord branch. Define the full `AnalysedSong` family of types: `AnnotatedChordSlot`, `AnalysedBeatSlot`, `AnalysedBar`, `AnalysedRow`, `AnalysedSectionItem`, `AnalysedSection`, `AnalysedSong`. Implement `analyseSong(song)` and a private `analyseSection` helper that walks bars interleaving tonality hints with chord slots to build key regions, calls `analyseHarmony` per region, then reconstructs the full tree replacing `ChordSlot` with `AnnotatedChordSlot`. Export all new types and `analyseSong` from both `index.ts` and `index.browser.ts`. Add harmonic analysis tests covering `AnnotatedChord.loc`, tree structure of `analyseSong` output, per-section homeKey, hint override and persistence, `{}` / `{home}` reset, and section-boundary implicit reset. See project/plans/ast-explorer-with-harmony-analysis.md for full context.
steps: Add loc?: SourceRange to AnnotatedChord interface; update annotate() and the borrowed-chord construction (~line 303) to copy chord.loc
Add AnnotatedChordSlot, AnalysedBeatSlot, AnalysedBar, AnalysedRow, AnalysedSectionItem, AnalysedSection, AnalysedSong interfaces to harmonicAnalysis.ts
Implement analyseSection(): walk bars collecting key regions split by tonality hints, call analyseHarmony per region, rebuild tree consuming AnnotatedChord entries in order
Implement analyseSong(song): map sections through analyseSection using song.key ?? 'C major' as fallback
Export analyseSong and all new types from index.ts and index.browser.ts
Add harmonic analysis tests for loc propagation, tree structure, per-section keys, hint override/persistence, reset tokens, and section-boundary isolation
Run: pnpm build && pnpm test

description: Add Analysed AST mode toggle to the AST Explorer page
detail: Add a `<select id="ast-mode">` dropdown to the editor toolbar in `ast-explorer.njk` with options 'Parsed AST' and 'Analysed AST'. Update the `update()` function to call `grigson.analyseSong(ast)` when the analysed mode is selected, passing the result to JSONFormatter and buildLocMap instead of the raw AST. Wire a `change` event listener on the select to re-run `update()`. No changes to buildLocMap or the hover delegation are needed — AnnotatedChordSlot.loc is picked up automatically. See project/plans/ast-explorer-with-harmony-analysis.md for full context.
steps: Add <select id="ast-mode"> with 'Parsed AST' / 'Analysed AST' options to the editor toolbar in ast-explorer.njk
Update the update() function to branch on the select value: call grigson.analyseSong(ast) for 'analysed', otherwise use the raw AST
Add addEventListener('change', update) on the ast-mode select
Run pnpm build, start the dev server, and manually verify: toggle shows AnnotatedChordSlots; adding {Ab major} shows tonalityHints; hover highlights source ranges correctly
